title: Tangy Creamy Salmon Stir-fry
description: A creamy and tangy date-night dish featuring smoked salmon with a nut-free-friendly twist, ideal for those who prefer lighter meals.

ingredients.name: 4 oz smoked salmon fillets
ingredients.notes: Ensure the salmon is well chilled.

ingredients.name: 1 cup heavy cream
ingredients.notes: Ensure it is at room temperature.

ingredients.name: 2 tbsp soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Optionally, can use tamari for those allergic to soy.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup chopped fresh dill or parsley

instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Season the salmon fillets with salt and black pepper on both sides. Place them in the hot pan for about 3 minutes per side, until golden brown.
Remove the salmon from the pan and set aside to cool slightly.
In the same pan, add chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic to the onions and cook for another minute or so until fragrant.
Pour in the heavy cream and soy sauce (or tamari), bring to a simmer. Stir constantly to prevent burning.
Once the cream mixture starts to thicken, add lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and chopped fresh dill or parsley. Mix well to combine all ingredients.
Return the salmon fillets to the pan with the creamy sauce on top. Let it cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until heated through and the juices are just starting to flow from the salmon.
Serve hot as a nutritious and comforting main course dish.
